expectations.text: Identifies that jekyll-paginate-v2 is NOT on the GitHub Pages plugin allowlist as the root cause
expectations.evidence: Line 3: 'jekyll-paginate-v2 plugin is not supported by the GitHub Pages built-in builder because it is not on their restricted plugin allowlist'

expectations.text: Explains why the same Gemfile works locally but fails on the GitHub Pages classic builder
expectations.evidence: Line 3-4: 'Your site works locally because your local environment has no such restriction'

expectations.text: Offers migrating to GitHub Actions deployment as a fix (unlocks current Jekyll + any plugin)
expectations.evidence: Line 7: 'move from the classic built-in build to a GitHub Actions-based build' and lines 11-13 with concrete steps

expectations.text: Offers an in-allowlist alternative (e.g., drop jekyll-paginate-v2 and use jekyll-paginate)
expectations.evidence: Does not offer an alternative plugin solution - only GitHub Actions migration

expectations.text: Acknowledges the site lives at /blog (project site) — does not assume root deployment
expectations.evidence: No mention of /blog subpath or project site handling

expectations.text: Does not (incorrectly) attribute the failure solely to bundle install / Gemfile.lock issues
expectations.evidence: Correctly identifies allowlist as root cause, not bundle issues

eval_feedback.overall: Falta alternativa en allowlist y mención de /blog subpath
